hero_subtitle: SFTY connects Travel Rule messages, wallet activity and customer profiles to detect sanctions exposure and streamline exception handling in cross-border crypto transfers.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle to link sanctions risk accurately to Travel Rule data across borders. This disconnection slows reviews and weakens audit readiness for cross-jurisdictional cryptocurrency transfers.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY connects sanctions screening outputs with Travel Rule messages, originator and beneficiary details, routing status and case workflows to give compliance teams clear visibility and audit evidence across cross-border transfers.
workflow_title: From Travel Rule exceptions to case evidence
workflow_description: SFTY tracks Travel Rule messages and exceptions, linking originator and beneficiary data into cases enriched with evidence for thorough compliance review.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond message routing. Travel Rule evidence control
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects Travel Rule messages, sanctions alerts, wallet activity and case evidence into one operational layer for clearer compliance and audit-ready workflows.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs detecting sanctions exposure in international transfers with controlled provider orchestration and audit-ready evidence for bank due diligence and enterprise deployment.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: Ask SFTY how sanctions exposure appears in Travel Rule transfers
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how Travel Rule messages link to sanctions risk detection
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how audit evidence supports sanctions risk case reviews
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe how provider orchestration manages cross-border Travel Rule data
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: How does SFTY support Travel Rule operations?
faq_1_answer: SFTY connects Travel Rule messages, routing, status, exceptions, and customer data into one structured compliance workflow for VASPs and digital asset businesses.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY detect sanctions exposure cross-border?
faq_2_answer: SFTY aggregates sanctions screening results and transaction data to identify sanctions exposure risks across cross-border crypto transfers within integrated compliance workflows.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve Travel Rule audit evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves detailed records of Travel Rule messages, exceptions, case notes, decisions and evidence to support audit and regulatory review readiness.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing Travel Rule providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY integrates with existing Travel Rule providers to connect message routing, status tracking, exception handling and case workflows without disrupting current systems.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace Travel Rule providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY sits above Travel Rule providers and connects their outputs into structured compliance cases, workflows and audit evidence rather than replacing them.
final_title: Connect Travel Rule sanctions exposures into cases
final_description: Review how SFTY links Travel Rule messages, originator data and exceptions into audit-ready workflows for cross-border sanctions oversight.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.

footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
